en.extract:
The sooty bushtit (Aegithalos fuliginosus), also known as the sooty tit, white-necklaced tit or white-necklaced bushtit, is a small passerine bird in the bushtit family Aegithalidae. It is endemic to central China, where it inhabits foothill and montane forests. Its overall browner plumage and white collar distinguish it from other small tits and bushtits. It is a social bird and forages in flocks, often with other bird species.


Taxonomy

The sooty bushtit was formally described by Jules Verreaux in 1869 under the current name Aegithalos fuliginosus. The generic name Aegithalos is a term used by Aristotle for some European tits, including the long-tailed tit. The specific epithet fuliginosus is Latin for "sooty", from the words fuligo and fuliginis, in reference to its dark brown coloration.


Status and distribution
The sooty bushtit inhabits mostly forested mountainous areas in central China, specifically in Sichuan, southern Gansu, southern Shaanxi, and southwestern Hubei. It is listed as a least-concern species by the IUCN.


References


External links

The '''sooty bushtit''' ('''''Aegithalos fuliginosus'''''), also known as the '''sooty tit''', '''white-necklaced tit''' or '''white-necklaced bushtit''', is a small [[passerine]] [[bird]] in the bushtit family [[Aegithalidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to central China, where it inhabits [[foothill]] and [[montane forest]]s. Its overall browner [[plumage]] and white collar distinguish it from other small [[Paridae|tit]]s and bushtits. It is a [[sociality|social]] bird and [[forages]] in flocks, often with other bird species.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sooty Tit ''Aegithalos fuliginosus''|url=https://ebird.org/species/sootit1 |website=eBird |access-date=25 September 2025}}</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
[[File:Aegithalos fuliginosus.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Illustration by Josephe Huët]]
The sooty bushtit was [[formally described]] by [[Jules Verreaux]] in 1869 under the current name ''Aegithalos fuliginosus''. The [[Generic name (biology)|generic name]] ''[[Aegithalos]]'' is a term used by [[Aristotle]] for some European tits, including the long-tailed tit. The [[Specific name (zoology)|specific epithet]] ''fuliginosus'' is [[Latin]] for "sooty", from the words ''fuligo'' and ''fuliginis'', in reference to its dark brown coloration.<ref>{{cite book | last=Jobling | first=James A. | year=2010| title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names | publisher=Christopher Helm | location=London | isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4 | pages=[https://archive.org/stream/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling#page/n171/mode/1up 165] }}</ref>

== Status and distribution ==
The sooty bushtit inhabits mostly forested mountainous areas in [[central China]], specifically in [[Sichuan]], southern [[Gansu]], southern [[Shaanxi]], and southwestern [[Hubei]]. It is listed as a [[least-concern]] species by the [[IUCN]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

zh.extract:
银脸长尾山雀（学名：Aegithalos fuliginosus）为山雀科长尾山雀属的鸟类，是中国的特有物种。分布于甘肃、陕西、四川、湖北等地，多生活于1000米以上的高山森林间。该物种的模式产地在四川宝兴。
